name: openrgb-python

metadata.name: openrgb-python
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: A python client for the OpenRGB SDK

documentation:
# OpenRGB-Python


[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/openrgb-python/badge/?version=latest)](https://openrgb-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)

OpenRGB-Python is a client for the [OpenRGB SDK](https://gitlab.com/CalcProgrammer1/OpenRGB#openrgb-sdk),
a manufacturer-independent, cross-platform way to control your RGB devices.
OpenRGB-Python can be used to interface with other programs, create custom
effects, or anything else you can think of!

I started this project because I wanted to be able to control the color of my lights based on temps. I tried to implement the features in the best (easiest to use) way possible.  

SDK Feature Support:
  - [x] Setting client name
  - [x] Getting device info
  - [x] Setting color by device
  - [x] Setting color by zone
  - [x] Setting color by led
  - [x] Setting mode
  - [x] Setting custom mode
  - [x] Resizing zones

Additional Features (Not part of the OpenRGB SDK):
  - [x] Loading profiles
  - [x] Saving profiles

# Installation

requires python >= 3.7

Use this method for the newest, but possibly buggy, package:

`pip3 install git+https://github.com/jath03/openrgb-python#egg=openrgb-python`

Arch Linux:
`yay -S python-openrgb-git`

For a more stable package:

`pip3 install openrgb-python`

~~Arch Linux:
`yay -S python-openrgb`~~
This AUR package is out of date. Use `python-openrgb-git` instead.

Thanks to @GabMus for the AUR packages

# Usage

```python
from openrgb import OpenRGBClient
from openrgb.utils import RGBColor, DeviceType

client = OpenRGBClient()

client.clear() # Turns everything off

motherboard = client.get_devices_by_type(DeviceType.MOTHERBOARD)[0]

motherboard.set_color(RGBColor(0, 255, 0))
motherboard.zones[0].set_color(RGBColor(255, 0, 0))
motherboard.zones[1].leds[0].set_color(RGBColor.fromHSV(0, 100, 100))
motherboard.set_mode("breathing")
client.save_profile("profile1")
```

# Documentation

https://openrgb-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/


For an alternative python implementation, check out [B Horn](https://github.com/bahorn)'s [OpenRGB-PyClient](https://github.com/bahorn/OpenRGB-PyClient)

functions.name: openrgb.utils.parse_string
documentation:
Parses a string based on a size.

```
param data

the raw data to parse

returns

A parsed string
```

documentation:
Packs a string into bytes

```
param string

the string to pack

returns

bytes ready to be used
```

documentation:
Parses a list of objects and returns them

```
param kind

the class that the list consists of

param data

the raw data to parse

the location in the data to start parsing
```

documentation:

```
param things

a list of things to pack

returns

bytes ready to be used
```

Packs itself into a bytes ready to be sent to the SDK or saved in a profile

documentation:
Applies changes in the color attribute

```
param fast

Whether or not update the device on each call

param force

If True, the function will update every led, regardless of previous state.
```

A parent class for RGBObjects that can control more than one LED like the
{any}`Device` class or the {any}`Zone` class.

documentation:
---
param address: the ip address of the SDK server
param name: the string that will be displayed on the OpenRGB SDK tab's list of clients
param port: the port of the SDK server
param update_callback: the function to call when data is received
---

classes.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.__init__
documentation:
---
param address: the ip address of the SDK server
param name: the string that will be displayed on the OpenRGB SDK tab's list of clients
param port: the port of the SDK server
param protocol_version: which protocol version to use
---

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.set_color
documentation:
Sets the color of every device.

```
param color

the color to set the devices to

param fast

If you care more about quickly setting colors than having correct internal state data, then set  to 
```

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.get_devices_by_type
documentation:
Gets a list of devices that are the same type as requested

```
param type

what type of device you want to get
```

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.get_devices_by_name
documentation:
Gets a list of any devices matching the requested name

```
param name

the name of the device(s) you want to get

param exact

whether to check for only a precise match or accpet a device that contains name
```

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.load_profile
documentation:
Loads an OpenRGB profile

```
param name

Can be a profile's name, index, or even the Profile itself

param local

Whether to load a local file or a profile from the server.

param directory

what directory the profile is in.  Defaults to OpenRGB's config directory for supported OS's (Windows or Linux), or falls back to using the current working directory.
```

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.save_profile
documentation:
Saves the current state of all of your devices to a new or existing
OpenRGB profile

```
param name

Can be a profile's name, index, or even the Profile itself

param local

Whether to load a local file or a profile on the server.

param directory

what directory to save the profile in.  Defaults to OpenRGB's config directory for supported OS's (Windows or Linux), or falls back to using the current working directory.
```

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.delete_profile
documentation:
Deletes the selected profile

```
param name

Can be a profile's name, index, or even the Profile itself
```

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.update
documentation:
Gets the current state of your devices from the SDK server, which is
useful if you change something from the gui or another SDK client and
need to sync up the changes.

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.update_profiles
documentation: Gets the list of available profiles from the server.

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.update_plugins
documentation: Gets the list of enabled plugins from the server.

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.show
documentation:
Shows all devices

```
param fast

If you care more about quickly setting colors than having correct internal state data, then set  to 

param force

Sets all colors rather than trying to only set the ones that have been changed
```

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.connect
documentation: Connects to the OpenRGB SDK

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.disconnect
documentation: Disconnects from the OpenRGB SDK

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.protocol_version
documentation: The protocol version of the connected SDK server

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.protocol_version
documentation: Sets the procol version of the connected SDK server

methods.name: openrgb.orgb.OpenRGBClient.ee_devices
documentation:
A subset of the device list that only includes devices with a direct
control mode.  These devices are suitable to use with an effects engine.

This is the only class you should need to manually instantiate.  It
initializes the communication, gets the device information, and creates
Devices, Zones, and LEDs for you.
